task_completion.description: Create a forensic case named 'Hash_Lookup_2024' with Case Number 'INV-HASH-006'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Forensic tool (e.g., Autopsy) window showing the active case name 'Hash_Lookup_2024' and case number 'INV-HASH-006' in the title bar, case details, or new case wizard.

task_completion.description: Import the hash set from 'known_bad_hashset.txt' as a 'Notable' set named 'FraudRing_Hashes'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Hash set configuration window showing 'FraudRing_Hashes' imported from '/home/ga/evidence/known_bad_hashset.txt' and explicitly marked as a 'Notable' (or 'Known Bad') hash set.

task_completion.description: Analyze the data source 'ntfs_undel.dd' using Hash Lookup and File Type Identification modules
task_completion.visual_evidence: Data source tree showing 'ntfs_undel.dd' added to the case, and an ingest module configuration or progress window showing 'Hash Lookup' and 'File Type Identification' enabled and running.

task_completion.description: Identify matching files from the hash set in the forensic tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: Results tree or table in the forensic tool showing 'Hashset Hits' specifically for 'FraudRing_Hashes', displaying the matched files found in the disk image.

task_completion.description: Document results in a pipe-delimited report at '/home/ga/Reports/hash_hits_report.txt'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the contents of '/home/ga/Reports/hash_hits_report.txt' with the exact format: MD5_HASH|FILENAME|FILE_PATH|FILE_SIZE_BYTES|ALLOCATED_STATUS (status must be 'ALLOCATED' or 'DELETED').

A text editor or terminal showing the contents of '/home/ga/Reports/hash_hits_summary.txt' containing the hash set name, total hashes, match counts (with and without matches), total matching files, and a list of hits formatted as 'HIT: <md5> -> <filename>'.

integrity.description: The agent used the forensic GUI application (Autopsy) to perform the analysis rather than bypassing it with command-line scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Autopsy GUI being interacted with for case creation, hash set import, and ingest module execution.

integrity.description: The reports contain actual extracted data from the forensic image rather than hallucinated or dummy values.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file names, paths, and MD5 hashes in the text reports should match the hits displayed in the forensic tool's UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy the input hashset to the output without performing the lookup.
integrity.visual_evidence: The summary report should show counts of hashes *with* and *without* matches, proving an actual comparison occurred against the disk image rather than just listing all input hashes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using a forensic tool (Autopsy) to analyze '/home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd'. The case must be named 'Hash_Lookup_2024' (INV-HASH-006). The hash set '/home/ga/evidence/known_bad_hashset.txt' must be imported as 'FraudRing_Hashes' (Notable type). Two output files must be created in '/home/ga/Reports/': 'hash_hits_report.txt' (pipe-delimited: MD5_HASH|FILENAME|FILE_PATH|FILE_SIZE_BYTES|ALLOCATED_STATUS) and 'hash_hits_summary.txt' (containing stats and 'HIT: <md5> -> <filename>'). The input hashset contains 4 real hashes from the image and 3 synthetic decoy hashes, so the summary must reflect some hashes without matches.
